result:
By default, React re-renders every child when the parent renders — props equality isn't checked automatically.

Common fixes:
- Wrap the child in `React.memo(Component)` so it only re-renders when props change by shallow compare.
- Stabilize prop references: inline objects/arrays/functions (`onClick={() => ...}`, `style={{...}}`) create new references every render, defeating `memo`. Use `useMemo` / `useCallback` in the parent.
- Move state down. If only part of the tree depends on the state, colocate it there so the parent doesn't re-render.
- Pass children as a prop (`<Parent>{child}</Parent>`) — `children` created outside the parent's render don't re-render when the parent's state changes.

Quick diagnosis: open React DevTools → Profiler → "Why did this render?" It'll tell you which prop changed (or that the parent rendered).
